title: RBI Liberalizes NBFC Branch Expansion Rules
description: <cite index="2-6,2-7">The RBI issued a regulatory update on April 15, 2026, aimed at improving the operational flexibility of NBFCs by removing the requirement for prior approval for branch expansion.</cite> <cite index="2-11,2-12">The RBI maintained a risk-based approach for NBFCs that accept public deposits, with their ability to expand now directly linked to Net Owned Funds (NOF) and Credit Rating.</cite>
ai_summary: RBL Bank has reappointed Deepak Kumar as Chief Risk Officer, strengthening its risk management framework. This leadership decision indicates RBL's commitment to maintaining robust internal controls and compliance standards.

impact.tele_collections: Minimal direct impact; however, strengthened risk oversight may lead to stricter compliance requirements in recovery processes and call recording protocols.
impact.field_collections: Field agents should expect enhanced monitoring of collection practices and adherence to RBI guidelines on consumer protection during field visits.
impact.legal_team: Legal executives will benefit from stronger risk governance, which may clarify recovery policies and reduce litigation risks through better compliance oversight.

compliance_action: Monitor RBL Bank's updated collection and recovery guidelines within 30 days; ensure all field and tele-collection teams are trained on any revised protocols.

course_module.title: Risk Governance in Debt Recovery: Understanding Banking Leadership and Compliance Standards
learning_points: Role of Chief Risk Officers in shaping bank-wide recovery policies and compliance frameworks
How strengthened risk oversight impacts collection team protocols and audit requirements
Anticipating regulatory changes based on leadership transitions in banking institutions
